title: 7 Rolls in Lago di Como
curator_statement:
Seven rolls of film shot at Lake Como in June 2023. Not a curated version of the trip — the full run of it, including the gaps and the frames that weren't quite intended.

The lake appears in almost every roll: from a ferry window, from above through a terrace of cypress trees, through an arch at the bottom of an alley, in the corner of a portrait. It's the one fixed thing while everything else — light, weather, which roll is loaded — keeps changing.

The B&W rolls and the color rolls are doing different work. The black-and-white catches the lake as infrastructure: the ferry as a commuter vessel, the architecture holding its age quietly, the water like hammered silver from the deck. The color rolls are warmer and more permissive — saturated ochre alleys, a yellow villa sitting right on the waterline, a woman in red stepping into a shop.

The double exposures in roll 6892 weren't planned. They're the best things in the set.

works.label: Double exposure — man in café, lake through window
works.curator_note: Roll 6892. Unplanned. Works because of it.

works.label: Double exposure — interior, burst of light
Como from the water, mountains behind

works.curator_note: Roll 6910. The quietest image in the set.

works.label: The lake, silver light on water
Woman on the ferry, squinting into the sun
